fields.name: RNGEN
fields.description: True random number generator enable

fields.name: IE
fields.description: Interrupt Enable

fields.name: CED
fields.description: Clock error detection The clock error detection cannot be enabled nor disabled on-the-fly when the RNG is enabled, i.e. to enable or disable CED the RNG must be disabled. Writing this bit is taken into account only if CONDRST bit is set to 1 in the same access, while CONFIGLOCK remains at 0. Writing to this bit is ignored if CONFIGLOCK = 1.

fields.name: ARDIS
fields.description: Auto reset disable When auto-reset is enabled application still need to clear SEIS bit after a noise source error. Writing this bit is taken into account only if CONDRST bit is set to 1 in the same access, while CONFIGLOCK remains at 0. Writing to this bit is ignored if CONFIGLOCK = 1.

fields.name: RNG_CONFIG3
fields.description: RNG configuration 3 Reserved to the RNG configuration (bitfield 3). Refer to RNG_CONFIG1 bitfield for details. If NISTC bit is cleared in this register RNG_CONFIG3 bitfield values are ignored by RNG.

enum_values.name: ConfigB
enum_values.description: Recommended value for config B (not NIST certifiable)

enum_values.name: ConfigA
enum_values.description: Recommended value for config A (NIST certifiable)

fields.name: NISTC
fields.description: Non NIST compliant two conditioning loops are performed and 256 bits of noise source are used. Writing this bit is taken into account only if CONDRST bit is set to 1 in the same access, while CONFIGLOCK remains at 0. Writing to this bit is ignored if CONFIGLOCK = 1.

enum_values.name: Default
enum_values.description: Hardware default values for NIST compliant RNG. In this configuration per 128-bit output two conditioning loops are performed and 256 bits of noise source are used

enum_values.name: Custom
enum_values.description: Custom values for NIST compliant RNG

fields.name: RNG_CONFIG2
fields.description: RNG configuration 2 Reserved to the RNG configuration (bitfield 2). Refer to RNG_CONFIG1 bitfield for details.

enum_values.name: ConfigA_B
enum_values.description: Recommended value for config A and B

fields.name: CLKDIV
fields.description: Clock divider factor This value used to configure an internal programmable divider (from 1 to 16) acting on the incoming RNG clock. These bits can be written only when the core is disabled (RNGEN = 0). ... Writing these bits is taken into account only if CONDRST bit is set to 1 in the same access, while CONFIGLOCK remains at 0. Writing to this bit is ignored if CONFIGLOCK = 1.

enum_values.name: Div1
enum_values.description: Internal RNG clock after divider is similar to incoming RNG clock

fields.name: RNG_CONFIG1
fields.description: RNG configuration 1 Reserved to the RNG configuration (bitfield 1). Must be initialized using the recommended value documented in Section 23.6: RNG entropy source validation. Writing any bit of RNG_CONFIG1 is taken into account only if CONDRST bit is set to 1 in the same access, while CONFIGLOCK remains at 0. Writing to this bit is ignored if CONFIGLOCK = 1.

enum_values.name: ConfigA
enum_values.description: Recommended value for config A (NIST certifiable)

enum_values.name: ConfigB
enum_values.description: Recommended value for config B (not NIST certifiable)

fields.name: CONDRST
fields.description: Conditioning soft reset Write 1 and then write 0 to reset the conditioning logic, clear all the FIFOs and start a new RNG initialization process, with RNG_SR cleared. Registers RNG_CR and RNG_NSCR are not changed by CONDRST. This bit must be set to 1 in the same access that set any configuration bits [29:4]. In other words, when CONDRST bit is set to 1 correct configuration in bits [29:4] must also be written. When CONDRST is set to 0 by software its value goes to 0 when the reset process is done. It takes about 2 AHB clock cycles + 2 RNG clock cycles.

fields.name: CONFIGLOCK
fields.description: RNG Config lock This bitfield is set once: if this bit is set it can only be reset to 0 if RNG is reset.

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: Writes to the RNG_CR configuration bits [29:4] are allowed

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: Writes to the RNG_CR configuration bits [29:4] are ignored until the next RNG reset

fields.name: DRDY
fields.description: Data Ready Once the output buffer becomes empty (after reading the RNG_DR register), this bit returns to 0 until a new random value is generated. Note: The DRDY bit can rise when the peripheral is disabled (RNGEN = 0 in the RNG_CR register). If IE=1 in the RNG_CR register, an interrupt is generated when DRDY = 1.

enum_values.name: Invalid
enum_values.description: The RNG_DR register is not yet valid, no random data is available

enum_values.name: Valid
enum_values.description: The RNG_DR register contains valid random data. Once the RNG_DR register has been read, this bit returns to 0 until a new random value is generated.

fields.name: CECS
fields.description: Clock error current status Note: CECS bit is valid only if the CED bit in the RNG_CR register is set to 0.

enum_values.name: Correct
enum_values.description: The RNG clock is correct. If the CEIS bit is set, this means that a slow clock was detected and the situation has been recovered.

enum_values.name: Slow
enum_values.description: The RNG clock is too slow

fields.name: SECS
fields.description: Seed error current status Run-time repetition count test failed (noise source has provided more than 24 consecutive bits at a constant value 0 or 1, or more than 32 consecutive occurrence of two bits patterns 01 or 10) Start-up or continuous adaptive proportion test on noise source failed. Start-up post-processing/conditioning sanity check failed.

enum_values.name: NoFault
enum_values.description: No faulty sequence has currently been detected. If the SEIS bit is set, this means that a faulty sequence was detected and the situation has been recovered.

enum_values.name: Fault
enum_values.description: At least one faulty sequence has been detected - see ref manual for details

fields.name: CEIS
fields.description: Clock error interrupt status This bit is set at the same time as CECS. It is cleared by writing 0. Writing 1 has no effect. An interrupt is pending if IE = 1 in the RNG_CR register.

enum_values.name: Slow
enum_values.description: The RNG has been detected too slow An interrupt is pending if IE = 1 in the RNG_CR register

fields.name: SEIS
fields.description: Seed error interrupt status This bit is set at the same time as SECS. It is cleared by writing 0 (unless CONDRST is used). Writing 1 has no effect. An interrupt is pending if IE = 1 in the RNG_CR register.

enum_values.name: Fault
enum_values.description: At least one faulty sequence has been detected. See **SECS** bit description for details. An interrupt is pending if IE = 1 in the RNG_CR register.

fields.name: RNDATA
fields.description: Random data 32-bit random data which are valid when DRDY = 1. When DRDY = 0 RNDATA value is zero. It is recommended to always verify that RNG_DR is different from zero. Because when it is the case a seed error occurred between RNG_SR polling and RND_DR output reading (rare event).

fields.name: EN_OSC1
fields.description: Each bit drives one oscillator enable signal input of instance number 1, gated with the RNGEN bit in RNG_CR (set bit to enable the oscillator).

fields.name: EN_OSC2
fields.description: Each bit drives one oscillator enable signal input of instance number 2, gated with the RNGEN bit in RNG_CR (set bit to enable the oscillator).

fields.name: EN_OSC3
fields.description: Each bit drives one oscillator enable signal input of instance number 3, gated with the RNGEN bit in RNG_CR (set bit to enable the oscillator).

fields.name: EN_OSC4
fields.description: Each bit drives one oscillator enable signal input of instance number 4, gated with the RNGEN bit in RNG_CR (set bit to enable the oscillator).

fields.name: EN_OSC5
fields.description: Each bit drives one oscillator enable signal input of instance number 5, gated with the RNGEN bit in RNG_CR (set bit to enable the oscillator).

fields.name: EN_OSC6
fields.description: Each bit drives one oscillator enable signal input of instance number 6, gated with the RNGEN bit in RNG_CR (set bit to enable the oscillator).

fields.name: HTCFG
fields.description: health test configuration This configuration is used by RNG to configure the health tests. See Section 23.6: RNG entropy source validation for the recommended value. Note: The RNG behavior, including the read to this register, is not guaranteed if a different value from the recommended value is written.

enum_values.name: Magic
enum_values.description: Magic number to be written before any write (0x1759_0ABC)
